data.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids and ERPR(-) and HER-2/Neu(+/-) Breast Cancer Prevention
data.brief_summary: The study aims to determine biological changes associated with a low vs high dose of omega 3 fatty acids, docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA), over 12 months in women at risk for recurrent breast cancer. The objectives of the trial are to develop mammary epithelial, adipose tissue specific markers of exposure and response to omega 3 fatty acid supplements that can be carried forward into definitive intervention trials of omega 3 fatty acids for breast cancer prevention. Inclusion Criteria:

* Prior diagnosis of Stage 0 to III breast cancer that is estrogen receptor negative, progesterone receptor negative with completion of definitive surgery, radiation therapy and/or chemotherapy.

endpoints: Changes in eicosanoids/metabolites including PGE2, PGE3 in breast adipose tissue
